data.title: Nova Lake Die Sizes Leak Sends Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Sliding
data.description:
Some interesting news slipped out recently about chip stock Intel (INTC), specifically about the Nova Lake processor lineup. Intel has a lot riding on the Nova Lake processor, which makes the fact that a leak emerged about these chips all the more unsettling. The news that slipped out focused on die sizes, and gave an advance look at the Nova Lake beyond what we have heard so far. Investors were not pleased, though, as evidenced by a nearly 3% loss in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The new word suggests that the Nova Lake line will have eight Coyote Cove P-cores along with 32 Arctic Wolf E-cores. That means around 110 square millimeters, which is quite a step from Nova Lake's predecessor. That processor measured about 150 square millimeters. That, coupled with the fact that the Nova Lake will be made using TSMC's (TSM) N2 process, means that the Nova Lake line will likely not be inexpensive to make.

It will help that most laptop Nova Lake processors will be made in-house at Intel's Arizona fab. And given that laptop processors outsell desktop processors by roughly seven to three these days, the costs may not be all that egregious after all. Still, knowing what we know so far suggests that higher prices than expected may show up later.

Wedbush Likes It

New reports suggest a reason to like Intel beyond that, though, as Intel is looking to put a $100 million commitment into SambaNova Systems. This might not sound like a great idea on the surface, given how aggressively Intel has been pinching pennies of late. But Wedbush analysts suggest that this could be the start of something truly impressive.

Reports note that Wedbush is calling the move a "…meaningful step in reshaping Intel's role in high-end compute." Given that a lot of computing power is moving to artificial intelligence, and SambaNova has something of a presence therein, Intel's connection to SambaNova could fuel substantial advancement in the higher-end computing sector. The fact that Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan has a personal connection to SambaNova, however, may taint the deal a bit.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 20 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 100.12% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.32 per share implies 3.38% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: "One of My Favorites": Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Jumps With Jim Cramer's Nod
data.description:
Some might be inherently concerned by the notion that Jim Cramer of CNBC fame likes a stock. After all, there was a mutual fund once that traded on doing the opposite of whatever he recommended. But one of Cramer's latest likes struck a chord, and aerospace giant Boeing (BA) is that like. This was good enough news for investors, who piled in and sent shares up nearly 2% in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jim Cramer's feelings about Boeing came out plainly on his X feed, as he noted, "Boeing, one of my favorites in the trust: lotta chatter of big wins out of China and Saudi Arabia. Given that they are getting better and better and quicker at making new planes these could take the stock back to $250." All of which is true, of course; we heard about those Saudi wins not too long ago with Saudia, and there were similar signs that China might get involved, even as it struggles mightily to get Comac up and running.

Cramer is not alone here, either; several other analysts have come out on Boeing's side in the last few weeks. UBS, for example, hiked its price target from $275 to $285 per share, holding its Buy rating in place. UBS analysts were particularly pleased with Boeing's improved free cash flow, as well as overall production processes.

The Biggest Building on Earth, By Volume

An unexpected fact about Boeing emerged, meanwhile, as it turns out that Boeing is the current Guinness World Record holder for the largest building by volume. This building, the Everett Factory in Mukilteo, Washington, stands at 472 million cubic feet of space, and features a factory floor containing 98.3 acres. That is roughly the equivalent of 75 football fields.

The building is reportedly so large that it contains its own weather system. A tour actually runs through the building in question, and takes 80 minutes start to finish. This is also where Boeing keeps its Future of Flight gallery as well as several exhibits, like a mock-up of the Destiny module.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, one Hold and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 27.41% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $269.50 per share implies 11.79% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: OpenAI Just Rolled Out Its First AI Model not Powered by Nvidia Chips
data.description:
OpenAI just rolled out a new AI model that's powered by Cerebras chips. Importantly, this is the first time that it has launched a model built with non-Nvidia (NVDA) chips. The model, called GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark, is a lighter and faster version of its newest Codex software, which helps automate coding tasks. While it is less powerful than the full version, it is designed to respond more quickly. That means developers can edit sections of code, run tests, stop the model mid-task, or redirect it to a new assignment without waiting for long computations to finish.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This move comes as OpenAI continues to increase the number of chip suppliers it works with. Last month, the company signed a deal that was reportedly worth more than $10 billion to use Cerebras hardware in order to deliver faster AI responses. Around the same time, OpenAI reached a major agreement with Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD graphics processing units over several years. It also agreed to buy custom chips and networking components from Broadcom (AVGO).

Nevertheless, OpenAI made it clear that Nvidia still plays a central role. In a statement, a company spokesperson described the Nvidia relationship as "foundational" and noted that its most advanced AI models are the result of years of close hardware and software collaboration. The company also said that it will continue to rely on Nvidia as the backbone of its training and inference systems, while deliberately expanding its supplier base to include Cerebras, AMD, and Broadcom.

What Is a Good Price for NVDA?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on NVDA stock based on 37 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average NVDA price target of $260.38 per share implies 37.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Apple's Vision Pro Gets YouTube, but VR Demand Keeps Sliding
data.description:
Video-sharing platform YouTube is finally available on Apple's (AAPL) Vision Pro. Indeed, when the tech giant's $3,499 headset launched in early 2024, one of the world's most popular apps, owned by Google (GOOGL), was noticeably missing. Now, a dedicated YouTube app has officially arrived on the visionOS App Store. With it, users can watch everything from regular videos and Shorts to 3D, 360-degree, and VR180 content. The app also supports playback in up to 8K resolution on the Vision Pro M5, although it requires visionOS 26.0 or later to function.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nevertheless, the Vision Pro continues to face challenges. For example, the high starting price has been a major sticking point for consumers, and critics have raised concerns about the headset's weight, comfort, battery life, and limited app ecosystem. In response, Apple introduced an upgraded M5 version in October, which features improved battery life and a redesigned headband to make it more comfortable to wear. At the same time, Apple is expected to release a cheaper, lower-spec version later this year in order to attract a wider audience.

Still, the overall virtual reality market has struggled to gain mainstream momentum. Notably, Meta Platforms (META) currently dominates the space with its Quest headset, which holds roughly an 80% market share, largely because its device starts at around $370. However, even Meta has reportedly scaled back its digital marketing spending for headsets over the past year. Moreover, according to Counterpoint Research, the overall VR headset market declined 14% year-over-year. As a result, reports suggest Apple may now be shifting its longer-term focus toward AI-powered smart glasses.

Is Apple a Buy or Sell Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on AAPL stock based on 17 Buys, nine Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AAPL price target of $306.89 per share implies 17.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Down Today, 2/12/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are down by over 1% on Thursday as investors digest new labor market data amid continued weakness in tech stocks.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For the week ended February 7, initial jobless claims fell by 5,000 to 227,000, above the consensus estimate of 223,000. In addition, continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, increased by 21,000 to 1.862 million and were also above expectations of 1.85 million. The data exasperated labor market concerns, with the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announcing yesterday that the U.S. added a total of 181,000 nonfarm payrolls in 2025, down significantly from its previous estimate of 584,000.

Tech Stock Woes Continue

Tech stocks are also taking a hit, with Apple (AAPL), Amazon (AMZN), Tesla (TSLA), and Palantir (PLTR) all down by at least 2%. Within tech, software is noticeably weak on fears that AI will intensify competition and lower margins in the industry.

Despite labor market weakness, the odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the March 18 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) have dropped in recent days, falling to 9.8% from 23.2% a week ago on the CME FedWatch tool. The release of January's Consumer Price Index (CPI), a key gauge of inflation, on Friday morning could send those odds surging in either direction.

data.title: Cathie Wood Buys the Post-Earnings Dip in These Stocks. Should You Follow?
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made strategic portfolio moves on Wednesday, February 11, as revealed in daily fund disclosures. The ace hedge fund manager capitalized on the post-earnings dip in Robinhood Markets (HOOD) and Shopify (SHOP) shares, accumulating millions at a discount.

Wood also acquired shares of cryptocurrency companies Bullish (BLSH) and Circle Internet (CRCL) as these stocks remain pressured amid the ongoing crypto slump. Additionally, she bought shares of software company Figma (FIG), leveraging the current dip in software stocks.

Most of these purchases were made across the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK), the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW), and the ARK Fintech Innovation Fund (ARKF).

Here's How Wood Allocated $74M in Stocks

Robinhood Markets – Wood acquired a total of 433,806 shares of this brokerage platform for $33.86 million. HOOD stock plunged 8.8% on February 11 after missing Q4 revenue estimates, though earnings of $0.66 beat the consensus.

Shopify – Wood added 150,421 shares of the Canadian e-commerce portal for $17.89 million. SHOP stock dropped 6.5% yesterday, despite beating sales and earnings expectations for Q4 and announcing a $2 billion share buyback plan.

Other Notable Purchases – ARK Invest bought 364,134 shares of crypto exchange Bullish for $11.60 million. Wood added 75,559 shares of the stablecoin company Circle Internet for $4.37 million, riding a 3.2% dip in its stock price. Wood also acquired 280,138 shares of Figma for $6.32 million, while its shares declined 6.4%.

Why Wood Is Optimistic on these Stocks

With crypto markets still recovering from 2025's regulatory headwinds and software stocks facing AI-driven margin pressures, these buys signal confidence in long-term growth narratives despite short-term earnings misses, such as HOOD's revenue shortfall offset by profitability beats.

data.title: Tesla's Model Y Was Once Again California's Best-Selling New Vehicle in 2025
data.description:
The Tesla (TSLA) Model Y was once again California's best-selling new vehicle in 2025, its fourth straight year in the top spot. This is especially notable given the challenges that the EV maker faced during the year, including protests against CEO Elon Musk and a temporary pause in production as the company transitioned to an updated version of the Model Y. In fact, data from the California New Car Dealers Association showed that the Model Y outsold its closest competitor by more than 50,000 units.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In total, the Model Y recorded 110,120 registrations, far ahead of the Toyota (TM) RAV4 at 65,604 and the Toyota Camry at 62,324. The Tesla Model 3 also performed well by ranking fourth with 53,989 registrations, just ahead of the Honda (HMC) Civic at 53,085. That said, while the Model Y continues to lead the state, its sales have been declining each year. Registrations dropped from 132,636 in 2023 to 128,923 in 2024, and then fell again to 110,120 in 2025. At the same time, Tesla's overall sales in California have declined, falling from 238,589 in 2023 to 179,656 in 2025.

One important factor behind this slowdown is the end of the federal $7,500 EV tax credit, which had supported electric vehicle demand for years. Without that financial incentive, some buyers may have decided to delay or reconsider their purchases. Even so, Tesla still holds a strong position in the state. CNCDA President Brian Maas explained that the Tesla brand has status, especially in parts of the Bay Area, though he noted that this perception may be weakening amid political controversies surrounding Musk. Nevertheless, the continued strength of the Model Y and Model 3 highlights their overall quality.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 4.4% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri
